四、产品史：从充电器到第一个爆款

The first product you created was Anker's charger, right? Actually, the first product we made was a charger. And it's a... It was actually very shallow back then. It was very thin and had a built-in cable. It was only after 10 years that you saw the whole market move in that direction.

— Although we made it, our marketing ability back then was to bring a product to the user's face, so that the user can really understand it and like it. It's relatively weak. So, it's better to be good than to be afraid of the alley. After that product, there was no special explosion. Later, when our development team was built, we continued to make products in one style.

Which one is the first explosion in your eyes? The first one that exploded was actually a lipstick charger. It's about the size of a lipstick made of a single power bank. It's very convenient to put the product in a bag and can probably fill half the battery. So it's been around for a few million sales. That was in 2013.

2013, two years of entrepreneurship. Yes, two years of entrepreneurship. Were your company's revenue and profits good in those two years? Actually, from the beginning, the growth rate was very fast. I don't remember the numbers very well. I remember that in 2012, it was over 100 million.

I think the path we chose for Dongping is quite strong. You can see the demand on the left. On the right, you can find or customize a good attack. The bridge in the middle, you can build it yourself. There is no difficulty. So it should be quite high. So you are a team that is easy to find and business. I think we should say we are a very honest team. We always stand on our own ability to go forward and reach the point where you should be able to reach. We always say that we are a company that has just established a step. We will definitely work hard to build the next step.

So back to your question at the beginning, it's like the chip and the robot we have been using since the time of Charging Castle. This is actually a state that you will definitely reach after each step.

融资与上市

Have you considered financing in your business? Actually, our cash flow was positive at the beginning. Because every cycle can produce good profits. So we actually never had a real demand for financing. Our first financing was in 2017. At that time, financing was the same as today's deep-seek financing. It doesn't necessarily require money. Because we started from e-commerce. Every cycle, from purchasing to sales, actually has a good profit. So the company's whole cash flow has always been positive. We don't need financing.

What are the benefits of this? The advantage of the price is that students who get shares in their hands believe that the shares are worth so much money. Because when you tell them that the shares are worth so much money, they always think that it's not reliable. But today, a VC, a head VC, who was an ITG, made so much money to buy this share. When you tell him that the shares you sent him are worth so much money, he will easily believe it.

The strategy of deep seek today is actually the same. I don't think so. DeepSeq doesn't need money. We don't know. The big model companies need a lot of money. I don't think I'm right. DeepSeq needs a lot of money to train. Last year, pricing was necessary. There was no complete pricing in the market. But after the market went up like Zip, DeepSake students can also use the price of the stock market to set a price for their own shares. So I don't think the demand is that strong.

But you were completely financing the price when the demand for funds was not that high. Yes, we were the first non-compatible reference system. ……

This has been decided when you were investing. Actually, our last time was also very clear. We hope that employees can convert their stocks. Then, even if we chose China as the last place, we also think that in China, maybe employees can convert their stocks and get more revenue.

五、15年三阶段：1357人群论与5星品牌

If you divide the development history of your company into some stages in 15 years, how would you divide it?

I think the previous 10 years is a stage. In the past decade, we have been from one category to many other categories, from one channel to many channels, from one country to many other countries. It's actually a very natural process of extension.

1/3/5/7 星人群框架

First, we will divide all the user groups into four groups. We call them the first class, third class, fifth class, and seventh class.

  • 1星：The first class is the type of people who only want to spend the least money and buy basic things that are affordable.
  • 3星：The third class will buy the best cost-effective product.
  • 5星：5-star wants good, stable performance, good quality, willing to pay a certain price.
  • 7星：Finally, 7-star wants the most expensive and best thing.

For example, let's say milk tea. The cheapest milk tea is a cup of milk ice tea for 6 yuan. This is 1-star. The best price is 10-12 yuan. Then there are the stable ones, like the $15-$16 one, like the one from Hushang Auntie. The best quality tea is probably $20.

Let's take another category as an example. For example, cars. The cheapest four-wheeled car is a $40,000 QQ. So the car from the first generation is $40,000. The most expensive and powerful car is basically the Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Audi. The military price is around $400,000. So you find that the first generation is 40,000 yuan, the second generation is 400,000 yuan. In the middle, when it comes to the era of oil cars, you find that Biadi is about 100,000 yuan. Our Dazhong and Fengtian are basically at 150,000 to 160,000 yuan. Many times they use the same engine, the same nuclear plant engine. They sell 100,000 in Biadi and 16,000 or 70,000 in Dazhong. Is it because everyone buys it? Right? Of course. Then how would you view the people who buy Biadi? Right? In other words, how would you view the people who buy Biadi?

So you'll find it very interesting. When you look at the cross-segmental and abstract view, the people are divided into four groups based on their different consumption habits. Right, 1, 3, 5, 7.

So in the previous 10 years of our company, what kind of brand or product do you think we do? I think many of your products are high-end and mid-high-end. I got this impression in the last few years. When did it start?

Actually, in the first 10 years, many people would say Anke is an overseas Xiaomi. Right? If you put it that way, then we are a... Because Xiaomi, as you all know, is known for its cost-effectiveness. So it's actually a three-segment brand. At least before Mr. Lei's high-endization, it was like this. Right? So many people would say when we were an overseas Xiaomi, Anke was actually a three-segment brand.

但是真正在海外买安克东西的同学会知道安克东西其实并不便宜 对吧 它其实往往比市面上的军价可能贵个20%甚至30%的价格 这个背后是一个很好的品质在支撑的 就是你看安克的产品在网上的星级 一星到五星的尺度的话 我们通常都是4.5星甚至更高

So, in fact, ten years ago, we were a five-star company. We made the products of a very good quality and stable quality. There were certain stores, but not many. We expanded to many types and made such a company. This is also a inevitable result for the channel we started from.

Because you know when you buy something on Amazon, you will definitely choose the new and good stuff. Right? For a brand, of course, you have a choice. You can go to the screen, the order, and then you can go to the 5-star. But for Anker, me and Dongping, we always want to do things for a long time.

嗯 不然的话我们还不如回公司去打工 所以要把事情做长久 我们就觉得不能做那些就是 cut corners 就是违规的事情 越界的事情 所以我们一直不刷屏不刷单 当你在不刷屏不刷单的情况 你要这个产品能够做到4.5星的话 其实你就在倒逼你的产品经理和研发团队 把这个产品的品质做到特别好 所以你看到安克的产品就是品质很好 有一点点溢价 这就是前十年的安克 这是我们的第一个阶段

为什么海外先红？

I have a question here. Because everyone used to say that Anke is a company that is not very well-known in China but is very well-known overseas. Is this your subjective choice? Which year did you make this choice?

This is definitely a subjective choice. Because we started to choose Amazon as a channel. So countries like the United States, Japan, and Europe are actually the best in development.

中国的亚马逊后来应该已经关掉了。所以当我们今天从这个渠道长出来的时候, 其实我们的那些国家就会长得更坏一些。

— Why is it so late? Many people may think that you think China is too twisted. So I don't twist it. I just do overseas. I just do overseas quietly. In fact, China's consumer development did start after 1718. Because it's very simple. If you go to Taobao to buy a power bank, the earliest price may be 30 or 50 yuan. Until 1718, you see everyone's 这对更好的产品的追求才会越来越冒出来。然后这个时候一个相对5系的产品在中国才开始会有市场。

So you actually made a product positioning as a 5G product, but at that time it may not match the Chinese market. The first step it matched was the European, American and Japanese markets. So maybe this is also a subjective choice. In fact, these positioning were all proposed when I was analyzing the summary of the regulation.

选择品类的"血海"逻辑

At that time, you only know you want to do the product well, right? Then you find a good product on the network, which is actually a 5G product. So this is a big part of the channel you grew up with. You first chose Amazon. Why does this look like a blue sea? Is it? In that era, why did you find this business opportunity and didn't find it? That point must be a very blue sea. Then I have to go to the store to buy things all day long. Because I'm a person who is very lazy to go to offline stores. So I basically buy everything online. When I first went to the US to study, I would go to all kinds of places to buy things in 2003. But after a few years, you'll find that you're only going to buy things on Amazon. Because no matter what the choice is, the price, quality, guarantee, and effectiveness are all the best. So when you buy something, you'll find that you can't buy the best choice, the thing you want to buy the most. So you'll do it yourself.

Looking back, after thinking about it, Right? And with today's industry vision, today's strategic thinking, and choosing this category, I think with today's knowledge, I probably won't choose this category. Why? You don't like it? No, actually, because you find it very fun.

This world is very fair. We just talked about how we didn't go through any financial difficulties before, right? And we had a smooth time. But these past few years, we've been through quite a lot. How long have you been smooth? Until 2022. I've been smooth for 11 years.

Wow. So you didn't lack money for 11 years. And your product was smooth. And then it went public. Yes. It went public in 2020. And I've always felt like I could start and do better.

六、超级品类与浅海/深海战略

In your very smooth 10 years, how did you choose a product or a product? Actually, our product line is quite natural. We made a product related to charging at the earliest. After you made the charging station, you found that everyone was short of chargers and cables. So this cable was made. Then along the smart phone, you found that everyone was still missing, for example, headphones, right? Laptop with Bluetooth.

当时其实还有保护壳,只是那个品类我们做的不太成功。然后这样到2016年的时候,你就发现其实当时大家说就是物联网, 对吧,也就是今天所有的产品都应该带上IoT, 所以那很自然的就是你到IoT的这个品类里面。

有想过做手机,做汽车吗?

We subconsciously think that we shouldn't touch those very large categories. When we first did a systematic strategy thinking in 2020, we realized that behind this intuition is actually a logical system supporting it. We call it super-classes and medium-sized classes, also called deep sea and shallow sea.

If you open an industry, you will find that the size is particularly large. We call it super-class. In the consumer electronics industry, the mobile phone is 5 trillion dollars, the PC is 2 trillion dollars, the TV is 1 trillion dollars. This is the top three. The fourth is the tablet. The tablet is only 6 trillion dollars. So you can see that from the top five to the top four, the number of 6 trillion dollars is directly 10 times the number. This is what we call deep sea.

So today, in the deep sea, there are less than 5 trillion dollars. You can count out one or two hundred classes. Right, like what we're talking about today, a charger costs $51, a charger costs $51, and a headset costs $210-$310. And today, for example, the drone from Beilei, how much do you think it costs? I think it's a lot. The total sales of the Beilei drone every year should be less than $51. So it's actually considered a "money sea" in our definition.

Many people have misunderstood this point of view. People think that money means simple. In fact, our money represents its small scale. Because the market size determines the quality of the product. In other words, if you want to make a product of this quality, it will probably need a lot of people. In a relatively small scale, you will find that the investment in the security is not too big. On the other hand, today you find that whether you are making a tablet, TV, mobile phone or car, you are at least a team of thousands of people. You need to develop a team to make this quality better.

We have an example of Dezoptok. It's like if each category is a Dezhaupok tablet, right? The so-called "foreshore" is its blind spot in the category of millions of people living in it, right? In this case, if the whole game is played, your winning and losing will be in billions, tens of billions. This is the small category of the "foreshore" we talked about. The super-classes we just talked about, one blind spot may be in five billions. So you bring 500 million to the table to play, and then maybe you play a three-game or five-game game and you're gone. This is what we call a deep-sea taste.

In fact, in the past ten years, the earliest ten years have been going forward based on intuition, but have not repaid. It has been in 2020 that there has been a review and summary of the past stages, and then the pre-sea strategy was summarized, right? Yes, in fact, in the past ten years, I think looking back, as a CEO, ……

七、2020的战略反思与2022的危机

What kind of company is Anker in 2020? Back to our logic framework in 2020, there are four super-classes in consumer electronics, right? And then there are many small and medium-sized products. You see, in 2020, we actually made more than ten small and medium-sized products. OK.

Then we made a five-series in these categories. It should be said that in this category, we have a considerable part of products made in five-series. and a few of them were 7-seater. Our Anfang was very expensive in the US. So Anfang was 7-seater. There were some other brands, but we were a 3-seater brand. So we have made more than 10 categories, and we mainly focus on 5 categories. One category is 7, and several other categories are 3. So it's actually a relatively scattered company. It's not focused on where it wants to go and why it wants to go.

What do you think the 10 years ago, the company's frequency of direct action has to do with your personality?

Back to the CEO. We are still back to value creation. In the past 10 years, we were happy to create value for our customers. But in 2016, I went to Wuhan to study at a university. The second semester. Yes. I was asked if my taste would become a thousand-million-dollar taste. My answer is actually very scary.

If you look at the category of charging stations, it will not become a thousand-year category. It will probably die in a few years.

— 到你不买它了,中间的跨度也就十年时间。 对,所以我们当时觉得,那充电宝这个东西我们从一二年开始买,我们当时觉得可能也就十年到十五年的生命周期,也就是到二二年或者二七年之间,它就大家就不会买了。

So it seems like the time has passed. The power bank is not at the point of extinction. But we believe that the taste of the power bank will disappear. So it only made you feel a certain fear. So when you start asking about the strategy today, you will have fear of your own taste. In this case, in the middle of a few years, you will think about how we can do something that is 不会消亡的品类。

九、做芯片：从分治法到端到端

You've been doing a lot of things recently, but it feels like you're not done yet. For example, you released a new chip. How did you come up with this decision?

This was done three years ago, right? It's actually back to what you said. The values guide us to make the decision to make a chip. We have a good way of studying in the industry. So in 2021, we established a team to study. We started with In the sound field, we started by using some small models to solve the problem.

Because you know, in the process of sound reduction, there are usually several steps. The first one is called beamforming. It's to calculate where the sound should come from through a few pixels. The second step is to reduce background noise. The third one is to do sound reduction. The fourth step is to enhance life.

Ten years ago, these four steps were probably done through some mathematical models, right? Through some programs. In 2021, when we started doing it, we had turned it into some deep learning models. Some paper problems started to be solved with deep learning models. In 2023, we actually saw the chat GPT. And you especially believe that the future should be an era of "end-to-end solution" problems.

This is also a abstract. In fact, the first 80 years of computers were an era of solving problems using division methods. It was like dividing a big problem into many small ones, then dividing it down again and again. For example, when I was doing search, I could divide a search into many words. In fact, search itself is a word problem in natural language processing. Then the search problem is divided into many words, then divided down again and again. Then a group of people go in to solve each problem today. So you find that in the whole search engine, it may take a team of hundreds or thousands of people to solve these problems.

But when the GPT-1 was released, you could see very clearly that in the future, we shouldn't use the division method to solve the problem. We should actually start from the division method to the end-to-end. The end-to-end method, you find that it is actually the first thing to learn from the data. Second, after learning the basic model, we can enhance it in the real-world process. Data learning and strengthening learning are the ways we can learn in the future.

So this is something that you can see happening very clearly. In the field of bioengineering, we have to throw away the old programming codes and small models and change them into a big model. Of course, the size of this is completely different from the size of today's language model. When this model is running on a portable device like an earphone, you can actually... Our hardware conditions at that time, the best chip could run a model with a few hundred K parameters. There would be a huge signal. It means that this model can't run on an earphone.

Then you look down again, and you find out why. Basically, because of the sound application, it needs to be cut into many time frames per second, and then continuously process the sound of each time frame. Without processing a time frame, you need to encode dozens of tokens, and then complete the model's all parameters. This challenge made it possible to move all the parameters of the model from the memory to the NPU at every second. And this move itself was the biggest advantage.

So our challenge was to make a model that could do a few megabytes, and the performance was much better, a single-core model. But we found that it couldn't run on the earpiece chip. Because this model needs to be moved constantly in the process of hundreds of milliseconds of push. If it's moved too much, the power consumption will be high. So in the end, we can only reduce it to a small model of a few hundred kms, 300kms or 500kms. At this time, its performance will be greatly affected.

So back to values. At this time, you know you want to make a model ……

冯·诺依曼架构与"存算一体"

The general structure of the phantom 1.0 is a word called "divide and store". You put the program and the code into the memory and then you do the calculation in the CPU. You will find that the general structure of the phantom 1.0 and the method of solving the problem is called the division method. There are also software called program and data produced by the division method. These three actually constitute a very stable triangle.

When you go through to solve a problem, you write down the code and program, and then the other data. At any given time, you only need to run a small part of the program. So when you're computing, when you're constructing, you separate the computer and the processor, and then you put the program and code in the memory, and then you just put a small part of it in the CPU every second.

But you realize that in the era of the end-to-end, — At this point, you are still using a phonoid architecture. When you find that you have your parameters stored in your memory, and every time you need to put all the parameters into your CPU to calculate, the natural efficiency will be very low.

For example, it's a bit like you have all your knowledge stored in your left brain. In the past, when we were talking about the division method, every time we solved a problem, there would only be a small part of knowledge that was used. We would just move that part to our right brain to calculate. At that time, it seemed reasonable.

But today, because your knowledge or the way you solve problems is no longer very detailed, but it is a whole, a large model. At this time, when you solve every problem, you actually need to think about the whole person. or a large amount of energy in the left brain, and move it to the right brain to calculate, do you think it becomes unreasonable? It's a bit low-key. Right?

So, look at what the human brain does. The human brain is one. Right. In fact, the human brain is your storage today, all your model parameters, in your nervous system. And the same nervous system also plays the role of calculation. So, in this way, your brain doesn't move any parameters during calculation. In this way, naturally, you get a higher number.

So, if we turn a single-celled phonoid into a single-celled brain, the power consumption will drop significantly. Then we can run a big-spectrum model on a small device like the headset. How big is this model? Today, we only have less than 2 million spectra. But for a model that can run in a headset, it's already huge.

十、端侧模型：从大脑到神经末梢

目前我们看到一些小参数的模型,比如说昆的7B的模型,它的智能程度不高吗?

我们觉得未来其实你肯定是需要在很多个不同的尺度的上面有不同的模型的。

The billions or even tens of billions of models can be called super brains. They solve the most difficult problems in the clouds. From billions to trillions of models, the probability is to run on the edge. High efficiency can solve the problems at the scene in real time. Finally, the millions or even tens of millions of parameters models we just talked about, they may run in some senses and control organs.

I use the human to make the comparison. So if we look at it from the perspective of the deep nervous network, the brain is actually a deep nervous network. The brain is a deep nervous network of different levels of concentration.

Then, if we look at your eyes, your ears, your sensory organs, and your muscles, these control organs, are they all controlled by the deep nervous network? So, let's ask, do your eyes transmit all the parameters they see directly to the brain to process? Probably not. Right? So, your eyes must have a set of vision nerves. This set of vision nerves is responsible for processing the light signals you see, and then transmitting a small amount of signals to the brain.

On the other hand, does your brain directly control every cell movement of your muscles today? No. No. Your brain is controlling the whole movement of your muscles. But your muscles are controlled by a group of nerve cells.

So we think that this is probably the case in the future. Different sensory organs and control organs should have some related problems. Or some nerve networks that match its complexity. For example, how many parameters do you think your muscle nerve should have? How many parameters should your eye nerve and your ear nerve have? Should they be lower than your brain nerve parameters? One or two, or even more. Right?

Then their parameters are much more different in scale. So these small models should actually run on some independent hardware. Right?

Including the brain. Everyone thinks that today, the giant brain is actually a scale of hundreds of billions of life-saving parameters. 十一、真正的智能家居：可调节 vs 可自调

So we say that all products can be divided into three stages. The first stage is called the unadjustable stage. For example, your house is very weak. It actually has nothing to adjust. And then the second stage is that it has more than ten buttons. It suddenly becomes adjustable. But it's not intelligent. The third stage is that he can adjust himself. He can feel what you're doing and adjust it.

Let me give you another example. For example, a chair. An unadjustable chair is what we're doing today. An adjustable one is like a human body. Today, you may have more than ten places to adjust. You find that you're not good at adjusting. Secondly, you don't think about adjusting it. When your back hurts, you might think about it. So this is the stage where we call it "adjustable". ……

So you asked why it seems that in 2026, our homes are not yet real intelligent. The modeling ability is not enough. Not smart enough. There is no modeling on them. Right.

You actually want to achieve real intelligence, it needs three abilities. That is, perception, planning, and control. If you want it to be intelligent, it must have perception ability. It must be able to plan itself, right? And it must be able to control itself.

So today, actually, and there needs to be hardware below. Right. The sensor and the model capabilities should be like the products in our homes. 十六、护城河三层论：技术 → 品牌 → 人

Because human perception, as a kind of simulated animal, human perception is limited. When your technology touches the line of perception, you realize that your technology is not going to have much space to move forward. At this point, others will be able to catch up. So your technical advantages may disappear.

十七、组织：分层授权与全员AI

Is your company a bottom-up culture or a culture of capital? Today, we are actually a culture of "division authorization". In other words, each layer makes the decision to do each layer. And then the most important degree of down-authorization.

As a CEO, I decide the company's structure, our strategic framework, our ability framework, our organization framework, our talent framework, and how to design our returns. These are my decisions. When it comes to our business department, I also decide what types of products we will make, the large red-walled products. But how do we do it in this specific category? Most of the details are authorised to be decided by the product line.

十八、AI 原生组织：价值链不变，组织形态改变

You asked about the original hardware of AI. What exactly is the original hardware of AI?

You find that whether it's hardware or organization, we think it has some things that must be unchanged. I think we need to think about these unchanged things and think about how AI will change them. The organization produced in this way is probably the organization of AI style.

So back to the organization, what is unchanged? First, the value of the user. You may think that today's speech is the most high-level one. In other words, for a product host, it's not different to make the user happy and satisfied. So, creating a value chain for the user. As I said, you have to go through the process of creating the value of the user.

For example, I want to make a hardware product. I want to do user research first, and then I want to define the product. Right? After definition, there are some technologies that I may need to develop. There are some designs that I need to do. Right? Finally, I need to organize it into a very good product. Then I need to make the quality of the product well. I need to make the production well. And I need to buy a very good product. Then this is just to produce the product. After that, I still have marketing, sales, service. I still have a lot of things to do.

So after AI comes, will these value points disappear? No. If you make a product that makes users satisfied, you still have to go through these values. So we say AI will not change the value chain of the entire organization today.

If you look at the value chain seriously, you will find that it looks like a big organization. In fact, the value chain is completed by a small team. In software development, it is called SPARM. In hardware companies, we now use the SPARM statement. In other words, every value chain has a point that is actually a group that is being replaced. So let's say today this group will completely disappear and be replaced by AI. It will not disappear.

How will it be changed by AI? Maybe it will shrink. This is what we saw today. In the field of software, for example, a traditional SCRUM may have 8 to 10 roles. From user research to the end of the unit test, Today, AI has brought about a sense of unity. One person can play multiple roles. In this case, the number of squams will decrease. It may be a dozen-person-squam, but it won't disappear.

The hardware is different. AI is here today, but I can't let an electronic engineer draw a structure picture. He can't draw a code for a chip. Because these are indeed related to the physical world. It still has a lot of work to be done. A lot of experience needs to be accumulated. So you find that the SCRUM related to hardware and the physical world — It seems that people are defining goals, right? People are dismantling and completing the process. AI can assist you in defining goals, help you to dismantle the project and manage it, and ultimately help you to complete a lot of work. So its efficiency will improve.

And today, it also reflects the cooperation between different scrums. In the past, people had to have meetings with each other. I believe that this traditional way of talking to people in the past would be more effective when AI came.

So to bring it back to what we just talked about, first, the value of the user doesn't change. Second, the value chain doesn't change. The third is the chain of the different payment organizations. These organizations probably won't change. But these organizations, first, some will become smaller. Second, the inter-organization efficiency between the internal and the organizational organization will be higher. So we think that an organization like this, a transformation organization like this, is an AI-era organization.

You mean transformation and progress? Progress and performance? I think after a year and a half of practice, we've seen the path very clearly. But to be honest, the transformation of people and organizations all need a process. I hope we can complete this transformation by the end of the year. That would be great.

中层会消失吗？

Some people think that the middle management of the AI era has become more important. Others think that the middle management will disappear. What do you think? Some people think that the AI era doesn't need middle management. For example, Jack Dorsey. He said in a famous article that he thinks he should use the world model to control this company and eliminate middle managers.

I'm being honest. I think the length of the context that the model can handle today is not enough. The length of the context is that you have to describe a problem clearly. the number of tokens needed.

For example, I have a project today. I need to explain how many tokens it needs. I have a scrum. This scrum may run several different projects at the same time. How many tokens does this scrum need? And today, I have about three scrums in my department. So today, the manager of this department needs how many contacts? If you build up the whole thing, you will find that the entire company needs how many contacts? After you've done that, you understand that if you explain to a company that the context length it needs today must be more than 1 billion, or even more than 100 billion, or even 1 billion to 10 billion. But it depends on the company's scale. In this case, with today's model processing capacity, what happens when it exceeds 1 billion context? It will either lose. Yes, today you can say it's compressed, but what's left in the compression? What's left? This is actually very, very important. The choice of value. Today's choice of value, I think the model is still not very good.

So in today's process, people will need to do a lot of complex context to grasp the key. And then another point is the relationship between the organization and the result of the organization. There is a famous definition called Conway's definition. Conway's definition is that the shape of your organization determines the results and the effect of the production. So who defines the organizational structure today? The CEO or the one who grows up.

You'll find that after the organizational structure grows, it's still organized by people. So today, let's say a high-quality organization. It has the right people, the right atmosphere, and the right way of communication. Can we say that the CEO alone supports it to grow? — I don't think so. — It certainly isn't. There must be a certain level of managers who put in the effort to cultivate this organization. So we can't assume that this company will have all the right people and they will work together to create the right people, work together, and the right organization. This requires the managers to put in a lot of effort and effort.

So I think that the middle-level managers won't disappear because of AI's arrival. And in fact, they may become more powerful because of the arrival of AI.

What will disappear? I hope that those boring jobs will disappear. The number of job openings will decrease. Yes, there is a saying today. People think that AI has eliminated job openings, so there will be no more high-level jobs in the future. The assumption is that high-level jobs will definitely grow from job openings.

Let me give you an example. For example, architecture. The first-class position of an architect is probably to build walls and paint the walls. There are many operational jobs. The highest position is probably architecture design and planning. If you look at ancient times, the architects were really gradually born from the craftsmen of the first line. When you become an architect, you probably have done a lot of work in the past decade to build walls and paint the walls. But look at modern architecture. It has become a certain type of architecture. You don't need to build walls and paint the walls for years to become an architect.

So we think that the future is likely to be like this. You don't need to learn programming in a certain field or adjust a lot of bugs to be able to be a programmer and build a solution and deliver value to your clients. In fact, we see it today. Today, many students who use AI in their daily life have no programming ability. I should say that they don't have any programming experience. They didn't learn programming from the fans. They didn't write the program. Today, they use AI to support them in their clear business field to deliver a complete system. to support the operation of the business. Our company has many examples like this.

So this is actually completely beyond the traditional "first-class" role, and it's now a "high-class" role. So I don't worry that after AI eliminates the "first-class" role, there won't be any "high-class" talents in the future.

N倍效能与"创造者拿七"

Do you think the organization will shrink? I think the organization will indeed become more experienced and more efficient. Will a minority earn a higher salary, like 10 times?

Yes, we are also talking about this. First, we define a term called "N倍效能个人". We call it "N-time efficiency worker". After that, it is "New". "N-time efficiency worker" is a new person. In other words, it is a person whose efficiency has been raised to N倍.

After looking at it, you will find that there are actually two groups of people in it. A group of people who use AI to build their abilities. In other words, these people are actually in the business to make the business's cognitive data flow into a method, and to generate the AI. They are the ones who create the sub-systems. Then, another group of people, we call them small-tongued people. We call these people big-tongued people. Another group of people who use AI to create AI are called small-tongued people. They may not have to make the smart objects themselves, but they can effectively use and control them. Their efficiency will be doubled because of the existence of these smart objects.

So we say that the big people and the small people are the ones who create AI intelligence, and the small people are the ones who use AI intelligence. So we think that in the end, this organization will have a small number of big people and more small people today.

And then the distribution in this, because their value is created by N倍. So a good organization should also distribute in a way that is equivalent to value. Because we do a consumer electronics company, we actually go back to the first letter. We say who created the value of consumer electronics? The people who make the products.

Yes, in fact, because of consumer electronics, we just talked about the speed of life and death, right? In fact, your product It's really not about the speed. If you can't make the most advanced product, no matter how great your company is, it might not work in a few years. Even if it's gone, Nokia is a very good example. So we say that consumer electronics, because you have to keep innovation, keep moving forward, and these must come from your employees. So today we say that its value is created by employees.

We think that a company that can continue to succeed, its value allocation must also respect the value created by this kind of value. So we think that more value should be given to employees, to these creators. So we have always said that the creator gets seven, and the shareholder gets three.

So we have thought about this question seriously. In the age of AI, when our value creation was raised by N times, we thought that people, creators, were still the main subject of value creation. So in the age of AI, we still insist on this kind of division ratio of workers creating and getting big heads.

Why is it 7:3, not 8:2? 9:1, 6:4? Actually, first of all, we would say that creators get more heads. Right? This means that creators get big heads. So today, Between 7.3 and 8.2, I'll talk about a bottom line. We define a term called "all-in-one value". All-in-one value is the amount of money you have to pay to get rid of all the money you have to pay today. And then all the rest can be divided into employees and shareholders. This is called the value of the overall profit.

It's interesting to see that the first-class companies, the most innovative and the most upright companies, usually have a value of 30% of the overall profit. So how do you divide the employees and shareholders today? How many shareholders do you divide? It's basically more reasonable. I don't think so. If a company's net profit is only 3.5 points, then this company is not a good company. The share price of shareholders should be 10 points. This is what we think is the foundation of a good company. So from this perspective, when you have a 30-point share price and the share price is 10 points, it's almost 7:3.

So when you become a creator, how much money do you get in return? Can you give us a summary? Actually, we're still calculating how much money we make. First of all, it's an average number. And we see the changes in the average number. Because of what we just said, we insist on creating better products. When we started to move towards the 7th generation, the higher the proportion of your products, the higher the value of your clients you created. So in fact, the total value of your clients you can keep is getting higher and higher.

So in the past few years, our profit margin has increased by 1 to 1.5 points per year. After higher income and higher profit, you can see that the share of our shareholders hasn't changed much. So the extra money is actually given to our employees. So in the past few years, our employees' income has been constantly rising. So our internal statistics show that on January 1, 2023, the company's total income in 2024 will be an average of 20% higher than that in 2023. And in 2025 and 2024, the same average will be 20% higher. So this shows that when your company's products are more advanced and innovative, your customer value will increase. When we have more value, our employees are more divided.

Back to the absolute number, we will also count the number of people who have earned millions of dollars in a year. In fact, in 2024, our company had about 500 people.

十九、AI改造的三件事：人、智能体、组织

You mentioned that we are facing a huge wave of AI, and everyone is doing AI variations. What do you think you did today? How painful is it? I just shared that we all use AI, and then we use it in our own mediums, right? Then we use it to integrate and use it, and then we put our business experience, knowledge, and ability into AI intelligence. This is actually a lot of work.

You understand, The foundation that AI can operate today is your data in the system. Today, the Internet companies may naturally have most of their data in the system. But today, as a hardware company, most of our data is not in the digital system. In this process, you have to work hard to sweep in most of the data gradually. Then, with this data, you can create the smart body that we just talked about.

And then, what's especially important is that today, smart bodies from different fields It needs to be able to communicate. In other words, if the data of the product's AI and the AI I developed are not connected, it's like you speak German and I speak English. It's not going to be smooth communication. So, behind this, we need to build up the AI in every field, and then connect the AI across fields. There's a huge workload behind this.

And in the end, you'll find that your organization will change. How? Your organization, as we mentioned earlier, you need to take out the scrum. These scrums need to be fully used by AI today. The changes needed behind this are huge.

So, in the end, your people will change. You'll find that among these three, the most difficult to change is people.

Should people adapt to AI more or should they change people? I think people need to believe that AI won't replace themselves. AI will make themselves stronger and willing to embrace AI today. And then give their own things to AI. Give some of their own data. Right. Actually, the first thing is that a small number of people will build AI. Build AI ability. A small number of people will build data, right? Build intelligence. And then a lot of people will use these data and intelligence applications today. Right.

So, Yes, we will summarize it in three ways. First, we talked about how humans embrace AI. Second, the ability to be integrated into the intelligence system. And then the communication between the intelligence system. Third, the change in your organization. So these three things are completed to become what we call an AI-era organization.

When did this organization change? Last year? We actually started last year in September. Oh. and define the structure, it will run smoothly. So from that perspective, I think you really start to understand how organizations should use AI. You must build your own intelligence in the field. So, Speckit is a set of intelligence that faces software development. Right? If you think about other fields today, when you can build that set of intelligence, your organization will become AI.

Has this person ever guided you to make AI organizational changes? Did you think of it yourself? We think we should be inspired. Like what you said, being inspired by the Spacket. But we believe in abstract. In other words, if you see something beautiful, or you see something It's a very surprising thing. You draw the underlying principle behind this thing. Then you have to spread from the drawing to various fields. Our way of organizing evolution is generally like this.

Did you become more anxious in the AI era? A lot of people around me asked me this question recently. Are you anxious? I think I'm a little anxious. Because today, it seems that its changes are very huge. And it's getting faster and faster.

But our habit is to ask what is unchanging. Like I just said, your customer value is unchanging. Your value chain is unchanging. And finally, your beautiful values need to be created by the creators of today. These things are unchanging.

Then you will ask yourself, are these unchanging things better or worse? we feel that we are becoming better and better at the bottom. I believe that no matter what changes we face, we should all be better. Of course, this is actually a rational way to help you overcome your emotions today. This doesn't mean that you don't have emotions at all. But I think in the end, you understand that rationality and emotions coexist.

AI Coding 与组织对策

AI coding is very popular these days. What impact does this have on companies like yours? We have a large software team. We have a software manager, a product manager, a algorithm engineer, a software engineer, a test engineer, and a computer engineer. I think there are 1,000 to 1,500 people. So how can we help people to get to the AI part? We think it's the company's responsibility. In other words, I don't want our colleagues to learn these things by themselves. And then, oh, it seems that they suddenly know.

From the perspective of the negative energy, we will internalize our own software intelligence system. Like the set of things in Stackit, it is actually very compatible with our product field, with the overall data structure of our company, and with the organizational structure of our company. And secondly, it will really help our colleagues to use the digital intelligence system today.

To be frank, a 5-10 year-old colleague who is about to change, his inner motivation is one side, right? The organization creates an atmosphere and an environment for him, which is also one side. So we think today, we definitely hope that our colleague who is in the negative can catch up with the AI today, instead of being pulled down by the AI.

Will you worry about being pulled down by the AI? I'm not worried. Because, objectively speaking, I think our company is really understanding AI. We embrace AI and allow our colleagues to be empowered by AI. I think we are the company that is leading.

Who do you think? You can't say that you are leading the big model companies. Of course. I think it's relatively similar to the consumer electronics companies in our industry. So actually, when Bear came over, you didn't have to run the fastest. You had to run faster than the same industry. Do you agree?

Do they have any actions for AI? We're not really trying to understand what other people are doing. You don't communicate with people in Shenzhen, do you? Yes, I'm a person who is in love.

But I think I found a reason to be in love. There's a book called "Live Game". There's a story in the book that's really fun. It says that back in 2004, Microsoft and Apple were competing for MP3 players. Apple's iPod was a huge success. So Microsoft wanted to do the same thing. The author happened to be in contact with the two publishers at the same time. The Microsoft publisher told him, "Look at our player. It's so beautiful. It's so powerful. It's better than Apple's. Then, when they talk to Apple's executives, they will say, "We think our users have some dissatisfaction. What are we doing to solve their dissatisfaction?" So, in the end, you can see who is the last person to go down in the hardware industry.

So I think this is a common basic principle. For companies that always want to talk about the idea of the first-person thinking, the principle of the first-person thinking is that when you look at the competition today, you will always end up with a faster horse instead of a real car today. So today, you don't have to compete day and night. You should think about the most practical needs of the users today. Or you should think about the value of the users today. And then look ahead and see the most advanced technology. and where they can be created. Today, when you develop the ultimate technology to create a product that satisfies the needs of the users, and you walk faster and more steadily along this path, I believe you will not fail.

I think it took us more than 10 years to understand this principle. Because when you're young, you always feel like you're comparing yourself to people around you. But at this age, I think we always say, "Hey, you've figured out the first-person perspective." So which year did you realize that you were a "no-no" person? Actually, we changed our mission to "2023." And behind that, there's another very real story. Because our company has always been... You'll think we're like... um um ……

And today, I think we are in many complex categories. Right? Today, we are doing something that is directly related to innovation and customer value. You will find that the first-person perspective we talked about is completely different from that time. It goes back to the principle of the essence of the business. We have to start making breakthroughs and innovations like chips.

二十、阳萌其人：工程师、稳健派、左手中庸

Did you want to become a 3C person when you were young? That's a good question. I don't think I'm the coolest person.

Then what kind of person are you? First of all, I'm not the person who says the most harsh words. I'm not the person who looks the most fancy in the brightest clothes. I think we've always been, me and Dongping, we've always been working hard, doing better every day. We are the people who stand firm on one platform and then work hard to build the next one.

So you're more like an engineer, solving one problem after another? Yes, I think engineers have more of this quality. They solve one problem, then solve a more complicated problem, and then solve the most complicated problem.

Are you a gambler? No.

Where are you in the left and right? Let's define left and right first. Let's say you have 10 million. You can invest in a project or a business today. How much money will you make?

What is the left-hand side? The left-hand side is the fist. The left-hand side is actually even more left-handed. It's a sense of extra leverage. You've invested your 10 million yuan, and you'll still borrow a lot of money to invest in this. So let's say the left-hand side is 5 points. Let's say 5 points is extra leverage. Then 4 points is probably the fist. Then if you count to the right, 1 point is probably not even a penny. Right? If you get 2 points, you can get 3-4 million. If you get 3 points, you can get 6-7 million. Basically, we have a choice of 1-5 points today. This is actually our way of thinking. So when we encounter a problem, we always like to find the right one. I want to find 5 dots here.

I've always been in the middle. We don't vote for everyone. We don't vote for everyone. We will definitely vote for those who are more than half. And today, we are going to the next stage. So when we said today, from 1 to 5, you chose 3 points, it looks like you are in the middle. There's a Chinese saying, "Chinese-yong." In the middle, you must be very mediocre. But I think if you put it in a long enough cycle, you will be able to make a stable investment every time. And as your awareness and ability improve, as your team becomes more and more creative, and your success rate becomes higher, I think it will eventually become a middle ground. In the middle, it will lead to success. Especially since it's a successful third-party company. It can support many creators and create many products on this platform. It can create successful businesses in many countries.

大疆与影石的对比

What kind of company do you think Da Jiang is? Da Jiang's number of products has reached dozens of products. So I think it's also a third-party company.

Is there any difference in quality between them? I think Dajang is a company that has been choosing the hard mode from day one, even the hell mode. The first day we chose the product category was a very difficult one. The previous years must have been very painful. When you beat the difficult category today, you have accumulated a lot of underlying skills. You have accumulated a group of high-value people. When you expand to other categories, it's easier for you to compete with them.

What about the Insight 360? — Right? You can either go 0 or 1. Of course, there's a degree of error in the middle. You might go 0.2 or 0.3. Or you might go 0.8 or 0.9. But in the end, I think the probability of difficulty and ease is at least one degree in the middle.

If you were on the same battlefield with Da Jiang, who do you think would win? I think we can still go back to the three elements I mentioned earlier. Right? First, do you have enough skills? Second, today, ……

I think in the EZ mode, you can do one or two things well, right? Then you can live well. In the male mode, you will find that you can't make mistakes in almost every field. So today you need a complete understanding and recognition of a company. And you still have to be able to make it work. So I think this is much higher than the requirement of being in the first position.

I think I've been down and out for the past few years. I've been self-reflecting and self-evolving. I've grown up to this day.

痛苦时的解法：冥想与价值观

You've started to practice huddle and meditation. Yes, I think it's painful sometimes. Meditation is a good way to solve pain.

What do you do when you're in pain? Today, when we want to make a company that can supply many types of energy, and many creators can make good companies, and today you feel like you are being beaten by the five beasts outside, it is very painful. But after a while, you realize that it is not unsolvable. Instead, you're missing the key part of the product you're building today. When you put these key parts in, in the next two or three years, you'll see that we're getting better and better.

The solution is cultural values, right? Yes, as I said, when you have a clear strategy, your organization's ability to generate electricity is leading. When you're willing to give a lot of money to people, Logically speaking, you think you can do it, right? Why not? In the end, you find that no matter how good your advanced methods are, advanced tools are all for people to use. And what kind of people are you dealing with today? What kind of behavior should they use to work in the workplace today? There is no clear definition. you realize that no matter how good the tools and methods are, they can't guarantee success. So when this part is added, I think everyone can see it today. In the past, we felt that we couldn't win. But now, we're winning.

This process and the transformation of AI are actually the same time period, right? The change in our value of the project started in 2023. The transformation of AI started in 2025. So it's actually a process of overlapping.

What is the relationship between your mission, your values, and your AI lab? As I said, we started doing two things in 2023. The first is to dig deeper into the technology of the vision. The second is to talk about the values of the mission and attract creators.

So you find a person with the spirit of creation, who has the idea of the first nature. He often looks at others and thinks, "This person is so strange." He always mentions some unusual things. He's very extreme. People think he's very stubborn. Long term, people think he's a KPI who doesn't care about other things. So, people like him are not very popular in many companies. He may not have a good experience in this company. But if you go to an environment and it is the environment that creates these things, and there are more people who do this, then people will feel happy about their work.

So when there are more and more people like this in the environment, it will actually change the people who are not like this in the environment. It's interesting to see if people will be changed by the environment. So when the atmosphere of our company changes today, it's not like you've changed people. When you've hired some great creators, the atmosphere has changed. In fact, the rest of the people are changing.

What details can you show us in terms of the environment change you felt when you went to the company a few years ago? As a CEO, what details can you show us? OK. ……

and it will not grow into a third party company. You can understand that, right? Because a third party company will rely on this system, on our creators, to run. It's not about pushing a step here or there. So today, we need to think about the framework. We need to find the key people today. Today, we really need to think about the framework.

让他们去把这个东西做出来的过程, 是我觉得就是我今天所看见的路径。

阳萌的一天

典型的阳蒙的一天是什么样的? 其实我会在我们不关心的关键领域里面, 然后可能开比较多的, I don't think so. I think there will be more discussions. For example, in the last six months, we have spent more time on AI transformation. From the organizational level to the specific tool level, do you think you will to go from the reality to the reality, and then go back to the reality to push forward more and better things.

What is your typical morning routine? What time do you get up? My typical morning routine is that I get up at around 8am, and then go to work between 9am and 10am. My meetings are usually not very long. I have about half to a third of my day as a meeting. ……

I know a lot about the field of knowledge. Oh, for example? What kind of knowledge have you gained recently? I'm actually more interested in things like politics, history, military, and also some of our hobbies, like food. These are things that I can spend a lot of time understanding. And you'll find that When you see a lot of specific things, and you try to draw it forward, there are many very close rules behind it.

What kind of commonality do you feel recently? I'll tell you something that's quite interesting. Every time the productivity revolution happens, some very interesting things happen.

First, you will find that the revolutionary revolution of the Communist Party of China is not a small progressive change. Every time the revolutionary revolution creates a higher value, right? This value will be distributed to whom? It will be distributed first. Military politics. You will find that the value created by this is often first distributed to people who distribute value. In other words, when the watermelon is cut, it's usually the person who cut it who takes it first. And in this process, you'll find that no matter if it's the first asset-based revolution or the one after that, it's always like this.

After that, it's usually through a revolution. and then we can make the value of the product more balanced. This AI transformation will create a huge value.

Are the people cutting the watermelon the model makers? I think the model makers are definitely a part of it. In addition to that, there are also people who use AI in today's enterprises.

So you see why Simon Altman said that in the future, we will enter a society called "general income". That is, the government will pay everyone. What is the complete solution to this? Today, AI will bring more value to the company. These more values need to be collected by the government in a tax-coupon way. Then, they will be sent to the government. So this completes a complete B-point.

So you want to make CEOs better? I don't want to say that we are better today. I always think we are honest and sincere. We hope to distribute value to people who create value. So in our company, I repeat to everyone, we said that we used AI today, we promoted it, and then the profits of the multi-products, in fact, everyone will not say that the ink is left to me. We still say that between the creators and the shareholders, they will distribute according to the amount we agreed on. We think that in the short term, it's good to distribute more value to yourself. But in the long term, when you want to make this value cycle run, you actually need to distribute value to people who create value.

读书与电影

What interesting books or movies have you read recently? I don't read that much these days. Because I've found that, from a context perspective, most books don't have that much information. Or it's hard to find books with that much information.

What about movies? Movies are a very fun habit. I can't remember the movies I've seen. I can watch it again in six months. It's a little new. In other words, when a person focuses on the present and the future, especially in the future, he is actually selective.

What kind of memory are you sensitive to? I think I'm persistent in my memory of structural things. When did this memory model come about? I saw you like to draw when you did a lot of interviews. Yes, you see, you have to get good grades to be a good photographer. You should learn to draw. Because you see, the so-called knowledge points and questions in college are actually highly repetitive. When you can take it to the next level, it's like a process of running a ice-cold-candy. You can finish it very quickly. So I think that's the first sweet spot for me.

And then, 持续的习惯就是好像从虚体的事情里边往后退一步,然后尝试去找事物本质的规律。

I think if an organization has only people who draw images, it's definitely not good for me. But if an organization has people who draw images like me, and people who have a lot of images, and they all work together, like I said, my MBTI is completely the opposite of our CEO, Dongping's MBTI. And then they work together, one plus one, it's actually getting better and better. So like what my company always says, if this company doesn't have me, this company might not be as challenging as it is today. It's more complicated and fun. If this company doesn't have Dongping, we should all die.

二十一、死亡假设与未来视野

If Anke dies one day, what do you think it will be? Let's go back to the principle of first nature. What do you think is the reason why a successful third company dies? — Right? In fact, the third type of company, the successful third type of company, when it can be able to maintain many categories and maintain a good person, this company will not die. The reason it died at the end must be because its people forgot about their previous mission and vision. Right? People no longer stick to their previous values. Then this company may not die.

What do you think about the results today? Are they good or bad? I think we haven't become a successful design agency yet. So I think we haven't reached the best of our abilities yet. Let's not ask for death.

If you don't catch up with the transformation of AI this time, will Anke die? What does AI mean to Anke? I think it's back to the value of users, right? One is the value of business and the value of employees. As we just mentioned, because of AI, the products around us will become real intelligence. If you can't provide such products to users, your company will die. And then the second is because of AI, your organization efficiency, right? The speed of human growth today and its ability to grow will definitely be significantly behind. If this happens, your organization will die. So I think today, whether it's the value provided to the client or the efficiency of the organization, or the growth of people, if you don't embrace AI today, you will die.

How long do you think Anke is a company that has survived? It's fun. There's an example. As a founder, How do you look at your company? The way to do it is to raise a baby. If the baby dies, you will definitely be in pain.

What about the way to save the baby? This is a work. There is a very interesting animal in China called pigs. If you raise pigs in the countryside, basically you raise them to eat meat for the New Year. So by the time the Chinese New Year comes, you will have sold the pig or killed it to eat meat. So as I said, the chicken is a pet, the chicken is a pig. And there's also a horse or a pet. So your horse is actually your partner. It's also your tool to a certain extent. Normally, someone would say, I want to buy your horse or your partner. You probably won't sell it. But if this person comes out today with a price that you can't refuse, you might consider this problem.

So from raising pigs, horses, to raising dogs, I think you first have to think clearly about how you view your business. And then me and Zhongping, we think we really look like we're raising dogs. We hope that this company can survive for a long time. Why do we think so much? Why do we work so hard to transform? We hope that this company will live longer than us.

招创造者：为什么不去大厂？

You said you wanted to hire a creator. Why didn't he go to the big company to teach? Objectively speaking, I don't have anyone I can work with at a big company. I'm sure you know that some of the real model makers at big company have a lot of income, but they have a lot of sense of crisis. Many people think that if they use a high-tech device for a year or two, it may not work after the evolution of AI. I believe that many people will have the same concern.

I think our characteristic is that we have contact with the physical world. So our time period is slow. So our human growth has a very long period and time. When I was at Xiaozhao, I told everyone today that if you go to a big internet factory, and a hardware company. Many people would choose to go to the Internet market in the early years. They would feel that they can make a lot of money at the age of 27 or 28. They would feel that they can make a lot of money at the age of 27 or 28. But look, the world is very fair. You always have to pay back the money you get. You will find that people will say that they are 35 years old at the Internet market. You will encounter a continuous problem. Because the next 27 or 28-year-old person will grow up.

Let's look at the underlying principle. The maturity of a person is based on the number of times he has fought in the war. Really? Think about a soldier. When he fought in the first few battles, he might have been very bright. But when he was in the 5th or 10th battle, he was already experienced. When he fought in the 15th or 20th battle, he was experienced and mature. So today, the maturity of people is highly related to the number of times they do it.

The key to Internet is that every account is fast. A product comes out today and improves, it may go forward in a few months. A feature may even be short to a month. So today, you find that a person who enters the Internet market, he may play one account in three months, and then play four accounts in a year. Right? When he was in his fourth or fifth year, he had already played more than ten games. At that time, he was already a mature and experienced student. So of course, his value has increased, and his income has also increased. But as I said, today, you find that when your game is played 50 or 100 times, the change is not that big. And when the people behind you come up, you will face a crisis at the age of 35.

But hardware is not like that. You need to make a product from a specific product. Nine months to a year is very common. Some complex products need to be made for a year, two years, or even three years. What does that mean? It means you need to fight for a year or two. Of course, AI will speed up this process, but it won't speed up to three months. So in the process, you'll find that you can continue to grow in a fairly long time. And your value is getting higher and higher. So you probably don't have to worry about not having a job today.

So what I'm talking about is that this point is also... This is a bottom line thinking. This is a bottom line thinking, yes.

But to be honest, today, first of all, you have to understand that the person who broke the upper limit and his proportion are very, very few. For most people, when you consider going online, you must consider going offline. And the talent that the big model can attract is actually very limited. Today, you will find that a successful model team can't be too large. So in this way, the whole world... I have always believed that there are many creators in this world. It's not that there are only a few people with special creative abilities. This world is set by them. We believe that there are many creative people in this world. These people deserve an opportunity to create.

大模型 vs 硬件公司

What do you think consumer electronics is fighting for? I don't think it's a war. Because at the end of the day, we still say that it creates value for the client. I think e-sports today, maybe among the products around us, you can see in your own eyes that it can create the most value-added products in the future. Except for models, except for these virtual things, right? We are talking about these actual, physical products. You can see in your own eyes that it can create the most value-added products in the future.

Do you think hardware is a big model extension? How do you see it in a big model? I think that the model is the brain that we talk about, the sense, planning, and control. The hardware is your hands, your eyes, and your many intellectual movements. Your body. Yes, so today you can't just have a brain, you must have a body. And your body will actually evolve with your brain.

The model company shouldn't cover this part right? — In the future, there won't be only trillion-level models, but also billions of models. These models will be deployed in distributed hardware today. They will be written in a virtual environment. So I don't think there will be a super-brain-controlled world.

But Hong An and Doubao both have mobile phones, right? They also have hardware. Yes, we just saw the leak of OpenAI's phone. I think that at the end, at the so-called "enter" point, I don't think it's just the entrance, but actually it's the data that records and collects people through the entrance. Because you think that today, the phone almost carries the data related to us, most of us, outside of work. Right? And for OpenAI today, I think what it probably wants is not the phone. What it actually wants is your data, your memories. In this way, its entire model can provide you with the best service. And today, mobile companies probably won't be willing to give it all of this data.

Today, Shenzhen hardware company has a model that is being trained. I think all mobile phone companies are making their own big models. Mobile phone companies are making their own big models. Secondly, we're talking about giant companies. They're probably making their own models. They're also making very big models. Giant models are all in the billions today. Three billion, seven billion. It's more like a small brain model.

二十二、推荐与结语：领导阶梯、TAO 与《星际穿越》

I'll let each guest recommend a book of life to our audience. Do you have one?

I would recommend a book called "Leader's Stairs". It's written by a person named Ram Chalan. Ram Chalan is the head of leadership development at GE. In other words, he is going to talk about how he has trained the talent of GE today. He has decades of experience and his long-term thinking. The book he wrote about leadership stairs actually divided the company into six different leadership stairs. It's very clear what each stair should do and what should not do. I think this thing is a huge guidance for me. It's like saying, "What should I do in my life? What should I not do?" And it brings a psychological explanation.

"语言与世界"与 T·A·O 模型

Our studio is called "Language and World Studio." When you hear this name, what are you thinking?

I think language is actually very interesting. You'll find that language is a very moral thing.

道呢,今天在英語翻譯叫TAO T呢,你的Task 就是今天你要完成的任務。 A呢,是一個一個的Action, 對吧,就是今天你每一個多說出來的詞 就是一個一個的Action。 然後O呢,就是今天因為這個Action的到來 然後世界發生了變化之後 你對世界的觀察。 所以,其實我剛講的這個, 也是我們很好玩自己的一個討論的一個認知。

那麼, Every time you speak, you say a single word. The word itself has no meaning, or the word itself has many meanings. The reason it has a precise meaning and changes in the world is that it is added to the words that were said before. It creates a new meaning.

So you find that actually, text and language can naturally make you observe how each token affects the whole state under a task. and what kind of token is needed for the next state. It allows you to start from a task and then push the next action from the next state. And how the next action will push the next state.

So what I just said is something that the students who work in the robot world will understand. Because there are two models today. The VOA model pushes the next action from the previous state. and the world model is what the world looks like from one action. Next state. Right?

So these two models are actually data that doesn't have a single data today, like language data, that allows you to observe the changes from O to A and A to O at the same time. So language is an effective whole of A and O under the task I just mentioned. So it's actually a very reasonable thing.

So we think that future robots will definitely need to have data and be able to define the double cycle of A and O, and finally train a model. This is a model that is very much in line with Taoism, very much in line with the T.A.O. model. This should be the foundation of future giant robots.

《星际穿越》

因为我们今天书架上放了一个《星际穿越》的黑胶唱片, 你喜不喜欢看这个电影? 我就很喜欢《星际穿越》这个电影。

为什么呀? 我觉得其实是人在巨大的挑战面前, 对吧? 你可以说其实人性的, Of course, there will be some negative human nature. Of course, there will also be some positive, very positive human nature. I think it's a very beautiful display of human nature in the face of a huge challenge.

At the moment of the change of AI, do you think it's like the feeling described in the book? I think you should always believe in human nature.

foreign Language is World. We hope to explore a new world with you. 也欢迎你在小宇宙的评论区,与我们有更多的互动。

观看原视频 →
item.description:
## 一、最反直觉的发现：阳萌的"非英雄主义"恰恰是他最深的护城河
整场访谈最让人意外的不是Anker要做芯片、做机器人，而是阳萌反复强调的一种"反创业偶像"姿态——他不是乔布斯型的暴君，不是马斯克型的赌徒，甚至明确说自己"不是最酷的人，不是话最狠的人"。他用 1/3/5/7 星把消费者切片，把自己定位成"中庸"的5分玩家。这看似平庸，但暗藏一个被大众严重低估的真相：
**在长价值链行业里，"中庸"是稀缺品。**
互联网产品的价值链短、节奏快，所以崇拜天才一击的个人英雄。而消费电子有200个角色、6000人协作，复杂度按"平方密度"上升——这种系统里，过于极端的CEO反而是熵增源。阳萌选了一种"无聊但稳定"的人格，让组织能跑长链条。这就像航空业不需要战斗机飞行员当机长——它要的是不出错的稳定，而不是英雄式的拉杆动作。
**跨领域连接**：这一点惊人地像《长尾理论》里的Costco模型——平庸的SKU选择者反而能跑出极致的会员忠诚度，因为他们的"无聊"被复利成了信任。
## 二、被掩盖的核心叙事：Anker的"芯片梦"不是技术升维，而是**对冯·诺依曼架构的哲学反叛**
表面

item.title: [邦妮區塊鏈 Bonnie Blockchain] 台股跌停板無情血洗！魏哲家：繼續買台積電就對了？台股市值超越南韓、印度的致勝關鍵！【邦妮區塊鏈】
item.dc:creator: 邦妮區塊鏈 Bonnie Blockchain
item.content:encoded:
💡 创造性洞见

一、最反直觀的觀察：台灣的勝利，本質上是「地理規模的詛咒」變成了「祝福」

大家都在歡慶台灣超車印度，但真正詭異的事實是——台灣贏，恰恰是因為它「太小了」。

印度有15億人口、南韓有5200萬，這些「規模」在過去40年是經濟學的聖杯。但AI時代發生了一件人類經濟史上很少見的事：「產業聚落的密度」打敗了「國家的體量」。台積電所在的新竹到台南這條走廊，騎機車3小時可以走完，但這條走廊裡塞著全球90%的先進製程、封裝、散熱、PCB、機構件供應鏈。

這就是為什麼Broadcom財報一打噴嚏、台股就重感冒——台灣不是「一個國家」在參與AI，而是「一個產業園區」被資本市場錯認成一個國家。台股42%是台積電一家，這個比例在任何金融教科書裡都會被標註為「極度危險的單一風險暴露」。但這同時也意味著：台灣不是在分散風險，而是在All-in一個賭注，而這個賭注剛好押對了。

二、跨領域連接：南韓「螞蟻」、明朝「東學黨」、與Reddit上的GameStop散戶——同一場全球運動的三個切片

影片提到南韓「東學螞蟻運動」很有意思，但作者沒挑明一件事：這是全球年輕世代第一次發明出一套「資本市場版的階級鬥爭」。

把三件事放在一起看： - 1894年朝鮮東學黨：農民用竹槍對抗日本軍隊和朝鮮貴族 - 2021年美國WallStreetBets：散戶用Robinhood對抗對沖基金（GameStop軋空） - 2020年韓國螞蟻運動：散戶用KakaoBank對抗外資拋售

這三場運動的心理結構是同一個：「上一代的階梯被拆了，但我們不躺平，我們找新的階梯」。差別只在於武器從竹槍變成了交易App。

更深一層的暗示是——南韓Hell Joseon、台灣的「躺平/小確幸」、中國的「鼠鼠文學」、日本的「下流社會」其實是同一個東亞病症的不同方言版本。但只有南韓把這種絕望「金融化」了，把存在主義焦慮直接導入了KOSPI。所以南韓股市的波動，本質上不是經濟指標，而是一個社會的集體情緒心電圖。

這也解釋了一個怪事：為什麼南韓人均GDP和台灣差不多，但南韓的加密貨幣溢價（Kimchi Premium）長年存在？因為他們交易的不是資產，是「逃離命運的可能性」——這個東西的需求曲線是無限彈性的。

三、隱藏的系統性真相：黃仁勛的「行程表」就是一份全球資本流動圖，而印度被剔除了

影片提到黃仁勛離台後飛南韓，這個動線值得放大十倍看。

過去兩年黃仁勛的飛行紀錄：台北 → 首爾 → 東京 → 越南 → 馬來西亞，但沒有印度。這不是巧合，這是一份AI時代的「新東印度公司航線圖」——只是這次的香料變成了HBM記憶體、CoWoS封裝、和機殼散熱。

這裡有個更冷峻的真相：AI不是讓世界更平，而是讓世界更「尖」。Thomas Friedman在2005年寫《世界是平的》時，印度是主角（IT外包、班加羅爾），預測知識工作將平均分配到全球。但AI幹的事情完全相反——它把世界壓縮成幾個「節點」：

  • 算力節點：台灣、南韓
  • 能源節點：中東（核電/天然氣）、馬來西亞（資料中心冷卻）
  • 資本節點：美國（Magnificent 7）
  • 稀土節點：中國、剛果

印度的悲劇是——它在任何一個節點都不在最前排。15億人口在「平的世界」是資產，在「尖的世界」變成了消費端而非生產端。

最後一個讓人脊背發涼的推論：如果AI第二階段是「Physical AI」（實體AI、機器人、自駕車），那麼下一個十年資本市場的最大贏家可能不是台積電，而是日本。為什麼？因為精密機械、伺服馬達、減速機（Harmonic Drive、Nabtesco）、感測器——這些「讓AI長出身體」的零件，日本握著比台灣更深的護城河。

影片暗示了這個方向（南韓的Physical AI），但沒有點破：台股這輪行情可能是AI第一階段的尾聲，而不是長期主升段的開始。魏哲家說「繼續買就對了」是站在台積電的視角；但站在全球資本流動的視角，下一個故事的主角可能正在輪轉。

📝 转录文本

開場：台股超車的歷史時刻與即將到來的血洗

2026年6月，台灣變成全世界第五大的股市，跑贏南韓，跑贏印度。不過即將到來的6月8號，也就是週一，讓市場超緊張的，台股可能要血洗一波了。

週五美股台積電的ADR大跌大概6.69%，這是史無前例，很少見。台積電一個人就佔台股大盤42%權重，所以大家預期台股這肯定是得崩的。

美股暴跌的三大原因

不過為什麼呢？6月5號週五美股跌得超兇。S&P500跌了2.64%，Nasdaq跌了超過4.18%，半導體股是一片血海。單日光是科技市值就蒸發了1.7兆美元。這又是為什麼呢？幾個原因。

第一個原因，6月5號發佈的美國5月非農就業數據實在太強了，新增了17.2萬個工作機會，這是遠遠超過大家的預期。失業率穩穩在4.3%。聽不懂啊。這聽起來明明就是好消息，市場為什麼要嚇一跳呢？因為這經濟表示很熱，聯准會可能就不會那麼快降息，甚至利率還要更高更久，higher for longer對不對。10年期的美債殖利率一下跳到了4.54%。高估值的成長股就立刻就被大家拋掉了。

第二個原因，半導體跟基礎設施軟體的巨頭叫作Broadcom，前一天的財報出來，AI的展望沒有大家期待的這麼爆炸。所以Nvidia就跌了大概6.2%，Broadcom跌了將近8%。這就是大家已經對AI的預期放得太高了，所以任何低於這個很高預期的數字都不算合格。

再來第三個原因呢，就是很多人箭指SpaceX的IPO即將要上市，它會吸光市場上的錢。這個IPO預計是在6月12號登場。估值高達1.75兆美元。這也是很多人擔心的重點。

歷史回望：台股的震盪基因

那我們回頭看一下台灣過去股市的歷史。這樣子跟美股連動然後大跌已經不是第一次了嘛。

  • 2024年8月5號：美股科技股拋售。台股單日就暴跌了8.4%，超過400檔股票是直接跌停。
  • 1990年：泡沫破裂，台股也從高點腰斬再腰斬，最高的跌幅是來到80%。
  • 1987年：全世界的股災也是跟著重挫。

這些聽起來都很恐怖，不過呢，再挖掘得更深一點。這些震盪其實你可以把它當成短期的波動跟雜訊。AI的趨勢是完全沒有變的。所以長期來看，站在趨勢正確的那一邊，台灣真的算是很幸運了。

印度的失速：當人口紅利不再是聖杯

如果把時間拉回幾年前，大部分投資人世界上的人應該都猜不到今天這個結果吧。

因為那時候全世界最愛講的故事其實是印度。印度有15億人口，是全世界成長最快的經濟體之一。很多的國際企業都把印度當成下一個中國，很多投資人說相信印度會成為未來10年最大的成長引擎。

那比較一下你看台灣就2000多萬人，那南韓也就是5000多萬人口。不管是人口規模還是經濟量體，看起來印度都是要贏得輕輕鬆鬆。

但是現在這排行榜是完全變了，第五名台灣，第六名南韓，印度掉到了第七名。更有趣的是，台灣跟南韓股市，幾乎在同一個時間市值完成超車印度的股市。那印度的媒體就瘋狂討論到底發生什麼事情了。

我是邦妮，現在我們開始來聊聊為什麼全世界的資金都集中湧向南韓還有台灣。

AI的兩個階段：黃仁勛的航線圖

如果說台灣是AI第一階段最重要的受惠者之一，那黃仁勛離開台灣之後立刻飛往南韓，或許也就是繼續說這個AI的大計劃。也就是AI第二階段的正式開始。

你看第一個階段AI競賽，核心重是資料中心、晶片、伺服器、算力、基礎建設對不對。台灣剛好就在這個供應鏈最核心的位置。

第二階段可能不只是訓練AI了，還要讓AI走到真實世界裡面。比如自駕車、人型機器人、智慧工廠，到工業的自動化。黃仁勛把它叫作Physical AI，也就是實體的AI。這剛好就是南韓很擅長的領域之一。

我想跟你分享一個很有趣的東西，也麻煩你一下訂閱這個頻道。我們會隨時更新國際上面最新的科技經濟資訊給你。如果你覺得有價值也可以按個讚。

南韓社會解剖：地獄朝鮮與螞蟻運動

南韓有5200萬人口，但是有超過1億個活躍的證券帳戶。可以說南韓它自己說是炒股世界第二，很少有人敢說自己是世界第一了。

但是如果真的去了解一下南韓社會呢，你會發現它有點像是對未來焦慮的一種掙扎。上一代的韓國人人生劇本裡面，努力讀書考到名校，進到三星、現代、LG這些大公司，買房買車養小孩。人生大概就是一路順遂往上。

但是到了今天這劇本難以成立，首爾房價高到莫名其妙，很多年輕人覺得靠薪水不可能買。有沒有覺得聽起來很熟悉。所以很多韓國年輕人覺得如果只靠工作，永遠沒辦複製上一代的人生。

這種情緒甚至有自己一個名字：Hell Joseon，意思就是社會激進競爭像是地獄一樣。拼命努力完全看不到翻身機會。所以資本市場開始變成一個出口。

南韓5200萬人口裡面有超過1400萬個人有股票帳戶。平均每四個韓國人就有一個投資股票。而活躍證券帳戶數甚至超過一億，將近全國人口兩倍。很多人是開好幾個帳戶操作不同策略。盯盤已經是人生日常生活一部分了。

東學螞蟻運動

更誇張的是南韓散戶有個名字叫「螞蟻」。雖然渺小沒辦法像財閥那樣掌握資源甚至外資控制市場。但若數百萬隻螞蟻往同一個方向移動，力量甚至市場都不得不注意。

2020年疫情期間外資大規模拋售韓國股票。結果發生很有趣的事，好幾百萬韓國散戶不但沒逃跑，反而集體沖進市場買股。南韓媒體就幫這場所謂社會運動取名叫「東學螞蟻運動」。名字來自歷史上著名的東學運動，就是一群普通人聯手抵抗外來的勢力。不過這一次他們抵抗的是華爾街。

台灣與南韓的差異

當然我覺得很多大城市年輕人聽到南韓社會這問題也會心有戚戚焉。像台灣也有這社會問題，比如房價越高、買房難、不要說躺平懶、薪資也追不上資產價格。這些台灣一樣存在。

若硬去比南韓，這兩社會還是有差異。南韓經濟權力更集中財閥手中，Samsung、SK、Hyundai這些財閥深刻影響經濟體系。讓年輕人覺得人生起跑點比努力更重要。

第二點是南韓社會對槓桿接受度超高。甚至出現一個專有名詞「Yeong-kkeul」，意思就是把靈魂都榨乾了，把所有能借的錢都借出來。

很多人擔心若現在不上車，以後就永遠追不上，很fear of missing out，怕錯過。為何南韓人愛炒幣炒股，可能就是因為害怕房價續漲、薪水不給力，怕這一輩子努力還是留在原本階級。這不只是買股票，而是在買改變命運的門票。這就是南韓股市最瘋狂的地方。

工具推薦：Mitrade 平台

當然不建議以瘋狂方式追趕。但身為散戶，怎麼小額嘗試參與呢。

Mitrade擁有全球四大監管機構牌照。商品很多，外匯、加密貨幣、大宗商品等等都可交易。它不是在買資產本身而是交易價格變動。支援信用卡USDT出入金，不用先換美金。免佣金且槓桿可高達200倍，不過必需要小心使用。帳戶甚至有100萬美元保險保障。

在Mitrade可做多做空。門檻只要5美金。與TradingView合作提供策略參考。獲選2025年歐洲最值得信賴經紀商。

透過本影片資訊欄連結加入，最高領取1300美金贈金。賺了可提領，賠了平台下。想要嘗試可以先無痛練習，連結在資訊欄。

AI時代的資本重分配邏輯

因為AI改變資金流向，過去幾十年投資最愛買的是銀行、石油、消費品牌。加拿大受惠能源、英國金融、印度受惠人口紅利及消費市場。大家原本想法是只要人口成長，資金自然流向這些國家。

但AI出現後，市場關心誰能提供AI最需的基礎建設。看全世界最大的AI軍備競賽都是科技巨頭在發動。Microsoft、Google、Amazon、Meta今年總資本支出就高達7200億美元。很多新增支出都是跟資料中心伺服器有關。比去年增加超過3000億。
